channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Voices from coal country say closures of MSHA offices will endanger mine safety
item.description: Stanley “Goose” Stewart, who survived the Upper Big Branch coal mine disaster that killed 29 workers 15 years ago Saturday, calls a recommendation by the Trump administration to close three dozen offices of the Mine Safety and Health Administration across the country “idiotic.”.
item.dc:creator: John Raby And Leah Willingham, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hegseth is to travel to Panama for meetings following Trump's demands for the canal
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth will travel to Panama next week to meet its leaders following recent U.S. allegations of Chinese interference in the operations of the critical Panama Canal shipping lane.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration ends key grant program that helps communities prepare for disasters
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the Trump administration weighs the future of the federal agency tasked with responding to disasters, it is ending a key program that has been used by communities across the country to pay for projects designed to help them prepare for natural disasters like flooding and fires.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida bill ignites debate over parental consent for minors' healthcare
item.description: A parental rights bill moving through the state legislature is sparking heated debate over access to reproductive healthcare for minors and whether or not parents should have a say.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Washington and Oregon sue over Trump elections order, saying mail voters could be disenfranchised
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Washington and Oregon on Friday became the latest states to ask a court to reject President Donald Trump’s efforts to overhaul elections in the U.S., a day after Democratic officials in 19 others filed a similar lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans are debating a higher tax rate for millionaires: Report
item.description: Republicans are reportedly considering raising taxes on millionaires to help offset the cost of their tax bill.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Judge blocks Trump from dismantling agency that funds community groups in Latin American countries
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge agreed on Friday to block the Trump administration from dismantling an independent agency that distributes grant money to community development groups in Latin American and Caribbean countries. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth is to travel to Panama for meetings following Trump's demands for the canal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth will travel to Panama next week to meet with leaders of the Central American country following recent Trump administration allegations of Chinese interference in the operations of the critical Panama Canal shipping lane.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Billionaires Musk and Soros push Wisconsin Supreme Court race spending over $100M
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Spending by billionaires including Elon Musk and George Soros has helped to push total spending on the Wisconsin Supreme Court race won by the Democratic-backed candidate over $100 million, the Brennan Center for Law reported Friday. That is the most ever spent on a U.S.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dem AGs sue Trump admin over order eliminating several federal agencies
item.description: Twenty-one Democratic state attorneys general sued President Trump Friday over his executive order that aims to eliminate seven federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Bernie Sanders, Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez to visit Bakersfield as part of 'Fighting Oligarchy' tour
item.description: Vermont Sen. Bernie Sanders and New York Rep. Alexandria Ocasio Cortez are scheduled to make an appearance in Bakersfield as part of their "Fighting Oligarchy" tour.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congress has the power to halt Trump's tariffs. But Republicans aren't ready to use it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As stock markets tumble in the aftermath of President Donald Trump's sweeping tariffs, Republicans in Congress were watching with unease and talking of clawing back their power to levy tariffs — but almost none seemed ready to turn their words into action.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dem senator looks to hijack key Trump budget process with tariff challenge
item.description: Democrat Sen. Jacky Rosen will introduce amendments to the Republicans' reconciliation bill, specifically targeting President Trump’s tariffs on certain items and certain countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to give TikTok another 75-day extension
item.description: President Trump said Friday that he plans to give TikTok another 75-day extension as his administration nears a deal to avert a ban on the popular social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's how newly imposed tariffs will affect California products
item.description: President Donald Trump’s widespread tariffs could significantly impact California’s exports, such as automobiles, computer equipment and semiconductors, according to a study from the Public Policy Institute of California.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trade war escalates as China hits back with 34% tariffs on all U.S. goods
item.description: Analysts say the escalating trade tensions between the U.S and China will make a near-term deal to end the trade war "highly unlikely".
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch, MPR News

item.title: Senate up late voting on GOP tax breaks and spending cuts plan that's central to Trump agenda
item.description: The Senate is still plugging through a contentious overnight debate on a budget plan critical to Republican efforts to pass trillions of dollars in tax cuts and boost border security and defense spending.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking And Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democrat Rep Jared Golden says his party's moving in 'wrong direction' on trade
item.description: Rep. Jared Golden, D-Maine, is warning his own party not to have "knee-jerk" reactions to President Donald Trump's tariff plan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The job market remains incredibly healthy -- but the tariff storm could upend things
item.description: Employers added 228,000 jobs in March, showcasing a solid labor market. But uncertainty over tariffs and tepid consumer spending may weigh on job growth in the months to come.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley, MPR News

item.title: PHOTOS: Biden’s ‘ambitious climate goals’ go down in literal flames by popular American beach
item.description: A major part of a first-of-its-kind green energy project, touted by Biden administration, is now lying in ruins and polluting some of America’s beautiful ocean and seashore in Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: President Trump’s approval rating slipped to its lowest point during his second White House term amid his handling of the economy and the recent Houthi Signal chat leak, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: K-12 schools must sign certification against DEI to receive federal money, administration says
item.description: As a condition for receiving federal money, the Trump administration is ordering K-12 schools to certify that they are following federal civil rights laws and ending any discriminatory diversity, equity and inclusion practices.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Oz as head of CMS along party lines
item.description: The Senate has confirmed Mehmet Oz as administrator for the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS), making him the head of Medicaid at a time where its funding is at risk of being severely cut. The Senate voted 53-45 along party lines to confirm Oz.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration back in court for deportation flight hearing
item.description: The Trump administration's DOJ is set to defend deportation flights under the Alien Enemies Act of 1798 in a Thursday hearing.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Other DFL statewide officeholders break with Walz on return-to-office requirement
item.description: The attorney general, secretary of state and state auditor said they would keep existing telework rules in place because they are independent offices.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Unemployment extension for laid-off mine workers gets tangled up at Capitol
item.description: Two proposals include unemployment insurance benefit extensions. The Republican-sponsored one would tie the unemployment benefits to environmental regulation changes. A more-compact version has advanced, however.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis announces support as Florida Senate takes up 'chemtrails' bill
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis expressed his support for a bill banning what he called weather modification "nonsense" before it hits the Senate floor on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams to run as independent in mayoral election
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams, a Democrat, is planning to run as an independent in the 2025 mayoral election, he announced on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Elon Musk did a 'good job,' but it's time to go: Bill O'Reilly
item.description: Bill O'Reilly tells Chris Cuomo that Elon Musk has accomplished what he sought to do but should now return to the private sector.
item.dc:creator: Matt Spears, NewsNation

item.title: Deadlocked St. Paul City Council sends interim council member decision to mayor
item.description: There were four finalists who interviewed for the seat. Three council members are in favor of one candidate, while the remaining three support another candidate.
item.dc:creator: Regina Medina, MPR News

item.title: New bill seeks to alleviate burden on Florida citrus growers as orange groves disappear
item.description: As Florida citrus growers continue to face hardships, causing some to make the difficult decision to sell their orange groves, a new piece of bipartisan legislation is aiming to alleviate some of the burden.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Democrats outnumber Republicans by just 2 voters amid statewide switch to nonpartisan
item.description: After Nevada Republicans surpassed their Democratic counterparts in voter registration for the first time in nearly 20 years, Democrats have once again taken over as the state's top party -- with a voter registration lead of two.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
